pages.nursingStudyGuides.addressUnderlyi: Adresse underlying cause, coach slow breathing, provide reassurance for anxiety-connexe hyperventilation, ajuster ventilator paramètres if mechanically ventilated.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.alwaysDocumentT: Always document the temps the critique valeur was received, the temps the fournisseur was notified, the fournisseur
pages.nursingStudyGuides.anxietypanicAtt: Anxiety/panic attacks, douleur, fièvre, early salicylate poisoning, mechanical overventilation, hypoxia-driven tachypnea.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.areTheMostAccur: are the most accurate indicateur of liquide statut changes. A gain of 1 kg (2.2 lbs) = approximately 1 liter of liquide retention.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.assessForSignsO: Assess for signs of avc (FAST: Face drooping, Arm weakness, Speech difficulté, Temps to call 911). A-fib is the most courant cause of cardioembolic avc.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.below735Acidosi: Below 7.35 = acidosis. Above 7.45 = alkalosis. Normal pH with anormal CO₂ and HCO₃ = fully compensated.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.calciumAndPhosp: Calcium and phosphorus have an inverse relationship — when one goes up, the autre goes down. Always vérifier les deux valeurs together.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.causeWaterToShi: Cause water to shift from ECF into cells (ICF). Exemples: 0.45% Half-Normal Saline (½NS), 0.225% Quarter-Normal Saline. Use for: cellular déshydratation, hypernatremia. Caution: never give to patients with increased ICP (can worsen cerebral œdème).
pages.nursingStudyGuides.causesIncludeAl: Causes inclure alcoholism, malnutrition, diuretics, diarrhea. Signs: tremors, hyperreflexia, tetany, seizures, cardiaque arrhythmias (torsades de pointes). Infirmier: IV magnesium sulfate (with calcium gluconate at bedside as antidote), moniteur deep tendon reflexes, respiratoire évaluer, and urine sortie.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.causesIncludeDe: Causes inclure déshydratation, diabetes insipidus, excessive sodium intake. Signs: thirst, dry mucous membranes, restlessness, seizures. Infirmier: gradual rehydration with hypotonic liquides, moniteur for cerebral œdème, strict I&O surveillance.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.causesIncludeDi: Causes inclure diuretics (furosemide), vomiting, diarrhea, alkalosis. Signs: muscle weakness, leg cramps, diminished reflexes, cardiaque arrhythmias (flattened T waves, U waves, ST depression). Infirmier: oral or IV potassium replacement (never push IV potassium), moniteur ECG, assess digoxin toxicité risque.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.causesIncludeHy: Causes inclure hyperparathyroidism, malignancy, immobility, excessive vitamin D. Signs: "bones, stones, moans, groans" (bone douleur, kidney stones, confusion, GI symptômes). Infirmier: hydration with NS, loop diuretics, calcitonin, bisphosphonates.
Causes inclure hypoparathyroidism, vitamin D deficiency, pancreatitis, rénal échec. Signs: Trousseau
pages.nursingStudyGuides.causesIncludeRe: Causes inclure refeeding syndrome, antacid use, alcoholism, DKA traitement. Signs: muscle weakness, respiratoire échec, confusion, rhabdomyolysis. Infirmier: oral or IV phosphorus replacement, moniteur respiratoire statut.

Causes inclure rénal échec, ACE inhibitors, potassium-sparing diuretics, burns, crush injuries. Signs: muscle weakness, paresthesias, cardiaque arrhythmias (peaked T waves, widened QRS, sine wave modèle). Infirmier: cardiaque surveillance, administrer calcium gluconate (cardioprotective), insuline + dextrose, kayexalate, préparer for dialysis if severe.

Causes inclure rénal échec, excessive magnesium intake (antacids, laxatives). Signs: decreased DTRs, hypotension, respiratoire depression, cardiaque arrest. Infirmier: arrêter magnesium, administrer calcium gluconate, préparer for dialysis.

Causes inclure rénal échec, tumeur lysis syndrome, hypoparathyroidism. Signs: often asymptomatic; may cause symptômes of hypocalcemia (calcium-phosphorus inverse relationship). Infirmier: phosphate binders with meals, dietary phosphorus restriction.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.causesIncludeSi: Causes inclure SIADH, cœur échec, excessive hypotonic IV liquides, and diuretic use. Signs: confusion, headache, nausea, seizures (severe). Infirmier: liquide restriction, moniteur I&O, assess neuro statut, administrer hypertonic saline cautiously if severe (risque of osmotic demyelination if corrected too rapidly).
pages.nursingStudyGuides.chaoticDisorgan: Chaotic, disorganized electrical activité — no efficace cardiaque sortie. Immediately defibrillate. CPR until defibrillator prêt. Epinephrine + amiodarone per ACLS protocol.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.completeAvDisso: Complet AV dissociation — P waves and QRS complexes are independent. Wide QRS with slow ventricular évaluer. Urgence: transcutaneous pacing, atropine, préparer for permanent pacemaker.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.confusionMuscle: Confusion, muscle twitching, hand tremors, hypoventilation (compensatory — shallow breathing).
pages.nursingStudyGuides.consistentEcgAn: Consistent ECG analyse requires a systematic approach. Use the same sequence every temps to avoid manquant critique findings. The recommandé approach: Évaluer → Rhythm → P waves → PR interval → QRS complexe → ST segment → T waves.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.consistentPrInt: Consistent PR intervals with sudden dropped QRS complexes. Plus serious — can progression to complet cœur block. May exiger pacing.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.copdRespiratory: COPD, respiratoire depression (opioids, sedation), pneumonia, voies aériennes obstruction, neuromuscular maladies (Guillain-Barré, myasthenia gravis).
pages.nursingStudyGuides.criticalOrPanic: Critique (or "panic") Valeurs de laboratoire are résultats that fall outside the Plage normale to a diplôme that poses an immediate threat to Sécurité du patient. When a critique valeur is identified, the laboratoire must notifier the infirmier(ère) or fournisseur immediately, and the infirmier(ère) must take appropriate action. Échec to act activé critique Valeurs de laboratoire is a courant cause of indésirable patient résultats.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.dkaLacticAcidos: DKA, lactic acidosis, rénal échec, diarrhea (loss of bicarbonate), toxic ingestions (methanol, ethylene glycol).
pages.nursingStudyGuides.dyspneaConfusio: Dyspnea, confusion, headache, drowsiness progressing to lethargy, tachycardia.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.electrolyteImba: Électrolyte imbalances are among the most commonly tested sujets activé infirmier licensing examens. Understanding normal ranges, clinique manifestations, and appropriate infirmier interventions is essentiel for les deux examen succès and sûr Pratique clinique. Électrolytes are minerals that carry an electrical charge and are essentiel for nerve conduction, muscle contraction, liquide balance, and acid-base réglementation.
Électrolyte imbalances rarely occur in isolation. Hypokalemia often accompanies hypomagnesemia, and correcting the underlying cause is as important as treating the Valeur de laboratoire.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.expandTheEcfWit: Développer the ECF without causing liquide shifts between compartments. Exemples: 0.9% Normal Saline (NS), Lactated Ringer
pages.nursingStudyGuides.fluidAndSodiumR: Liquide and sodium restriction, administrer diuretics (furosemide — moniteur potassium), elevate HOB, daily weights, I&O surveillance, oxygène thérapie if dyspneic, assess poumon sounds frequently.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.heartFailureRen: Cœur échec, rénal échec, foie cirrhosis, excessive IV liquide administration, SIADH, excessive sodium intake.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.hemorrhageVomit: Hémorragie, vomiting, diarrhea, excessive diuresis, burns, third-spacing, inadequate liquide intake.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.iWouldLikeToReq: "I would like to demander [spécifique orders: calcium gluconate, insulin/D50, kayexalate, répéter lab in X heures]."
pages.nursingStudyGuides.ifHcoIsAbnormal: If HCO₃ is anormal and explains the pH changer, the primaire trouble is metabolic. HCO₃ is a base — decreased HCO₃ causes acidosis.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.ifPacoIsAbnorma: If PaCO₂ is anormal and explains the pH changer, the primaire trouble is respiratoire. CO₂ is an acid — elevated CO₂ causes acidosis.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.ifTheOtherSyste: If the autre système (respiratoire or metabolic) is also anormal and moving in the direction to normalize pH, compensation is occurring.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.improveVentilat: Améliorer ventilation (positionnement, bronchodilators, incentive spirometry), administrer supplemental oxygène cautiously in COPD patients, préparer for mechanical ventilation if severe, moniteur respiratoire évaluer and depth.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.irregularlyIrre: Irregularly irregular rhythm with no discernible P waves (fibrillatory référence). Risque of atrial thrombus formation → avc. Gestion: évaluer contrôle (diltiazem, metoprolol), rhythm contrôle (amiodarone, cardioversion), anticoagulation (warfarin, DOACs).
pages.nursingStudyGuides.ivFluidReplacem: IV liquide replacement (isotonic liquides for volume expansion), strict I&O surveillance, daily weights (same temps, same scale, same clothing), oral rehydration when tolerated, moniteur Signes vitaux for signs of improvement.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.knowThe77RuleFo: Know the "7/7 rule" for transfusion triggers — hemoglobin < 7 g/dL generally triggers RBC transfusion consideration. For platelets, < 10,000 = risque of spontaneous saignement; < 50,000 = saignement precautions needed.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.kussmaulRespira: Kussmaul respirations (deep, rapid breathing — compensatory), confusion, fatigue, nausea, abdominal douleur.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.lightheadedness: Lightheadedness, numbness/tingling (circumoral and extremities), muscle spasms, palpitations.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.monaMorphineIfN: MONA (Morphine if needed, Oxygène if SpO₂ < 94%, Nitroglycerin, Aspirin 325 mg chewed). Activer cath lab for PCI within 90 minutes of arrival. Obtain serial 12-lead ECGs. Moniteur for dysrhythmias.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.normal275295Mos: (normal: 275–295 mOsm/kg) reflects the concentration of solutes in the blood. It is primarily determined by sodium, glucose, and BUN. An elevated osmolality indicates concentrated blood (déshydratation), while faible osmolality indicates dilute blood (liquide overload or SIADH).
pages.nursingStudyGuides.normalSinusRhyt: Normal sinus rhythm is the référence against which tous autre rhythms are compared. Criteria: évaluer 60–100 bpm, regular rhythm, one P wave before each QRS, PR interval 0.12–0.20 sec, narrow QRS (< 0.12 sec). NSR originates from the SA node and represents normal cardiaque conduction.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.phGt745WithHco: pH > 7.45 with HCO₃⁻ > 26 mEq/L. The corps has excess bicarbonate or lost acid.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.phGt745WithPaco: pH > 7.45 with PaCO₂ < 35 mmHg. The poumons are eliminating too much CO₂ (hyperventilation).
pages.nursingStudyGuides.phLt735WithHco: pH < 7.35 with HCO₃⁻ < 22 mEq/L. The corps has excess acid or lost bicarbonate.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.phLt735WithPaco: pH < 7.35 with PaCO₂ > 45 mmHg. The poumons are not eliminating enough CO₂.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.potassiumIsTheM: Potassium is the most commonly tested Électrolyte. Remember: IV potassium must be diluted and infused slowly (max 10 mEq/hr peripherally, max 20 mEq/hr centrally). Never push IV potassium — it causes cardiaque arrest.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.prIntervalGt020: PR interval > 0.20 sec, every P wave conducts. Usually benign — moniteur only.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.progressivePrPr: Progressive PR prolongation until a QRS is dropped. Often transient and asymptomatic. Moniteur; usually no traitement needed.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.prolongedVomiti: Prolonged vomiting/NG suction (loss of HCl), excessive antacid use, hypokalemia, diuretic use.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.pullWaterFromCe: Pull water from cells into the ECF. Exemples: 3% Saline, D10W, D5NS, D5LR. Use for: severe hyponatremia, cerebral œdème (3% saline). Caution: give slowly; risque of liquide volume overload and phlebitis.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.rateGt100BpmWit: Évaluer > 100 bpm with normal morphologie. Causes: fièvre, douleur, anxiety, déshydratation, hémorragie, cœur échec. Traitement: adresse the underlying cause — sinus tachycardia is a symptôme, not a primaire rhythm trouble.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.rateLt60BpmWith: Évaluer < 60 bpm with normal P-QRS-T morphologie. Courant in athletes, during sleep, and with beta-blocker use. Treat with atropine if symptomatic (hypotension, altered mental statut). Pacing may be needed for unresponsive symptomatic bradycardia.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.rememberHypoSwe: Remember "hypo = swells cells, hyper = shrinks cells." Never give hypotonic liquides to head blessure patients (brain cells swell → increased ICP). Use hypertonic saline cautiously — too-rapid correction of hyponatremia causes osmotic demyelination syndrome.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.replaceChloride: Remplacer chloride and potassium, administrer anti-emetics, discontinue offending médicaments, moniteur respiratoire statut for compensatory hypoventilation.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.sawtoothPattern: "Sawtooth" modèle of flutter waves, typically at 300 bpm with 2:1, 3:1, or 4:1 conduction. Ventricular évaluer is regular. Traitement similar to A-fib; cardioversion is often efficace.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.stelevationMyoc: ST-elevation myocardial infarction (STEMI) requires immediate recognition and intervention. Look for ST-segment elevation ≥ 1mm in two or plus contiguous leads. Territory localization: Inferior (II, III, aVF), Anterior (V1–V4), Lateral (I, aVL, V5–V6). Reciprocal ST depression in opposite leads supports the diagnostic.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.tachycardiaHypo: Tachycardia, hypotension, orthostatic hypotension, decreased urine sortie (< 30 mL/hr), concentrated urine (spécifique gravity > 1.030), poor skin turgor, dry mucous membranes, elevated BUN-to-creatinine ratio (> 20:1), poids loss (1 L = 1 kg = 2.2 lbs).
pages.nursingStudyGuides.theBodyMaintain: The corps maintains blood pH between 7.35 and 7.45 through three buffering systèmes: the bicarbonate buffer système, the respiratoire système (poumons), and the rénal système (kidneys). Disruptions to this balance résultat in four primaire acid-base troubles that nurses must recognize and gérer.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.theLungsCompens: The poumons compensate quickly (minutes to heures) by adjusting CO₂ elimination. The kidneys compensate slowly (heures to jours) by retaining or excreting bicarbonate.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.thePatientWasAd: "The patient was admitted for [diagnostic]. Relevant historique includes [médicaments, conditions]."
pages.nursingStudyGuides.thePotassiumCam: "The potassium came retour at 6.8 mEq/L. The patient
pages.nursingStudyGuides.theRomeMnemonic: The "ROME" mnemonic: Respiratoire = Opposite (pH and CO₂ déplacer in opposite directions). Metabolic = Equal (pH and HCO₃ déplacer in the same direction).
pages.nursingStudyGuides.threeOrMoreCons: Three or plus consecutive PVCs at évaluer > 100 bpm. Wide QRS complexes. Pulseless V-tach: defibrillate immediately. V-tach with pouls: amiodarone, synchronized cardioversion if unstable.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.toPracticeStepb: to pratique étape-by-étape acid-base analyse with clinique interpretation.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.totalBodyWaterC: Total corps water comprises approximately 60% of corps poids in adults (less in elderly and obese patients). This water is distributed between two main compartments: intracellular liquide (ICF, about 2/3) and extracellular liquide (ECF, about 1/3). The ECF is further divided into intravascular (plasma) and interstitial (between cells) compartments.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.treatUnderlying: Treat underlying cause (insuline for DKA, liquides for lactic acidosis), administrer sodium bicarbonate if pH < 7.1, moniteur potassium (shifts with acidosis correction), assess respiratoire effort.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.vfibAndPulseles: V-fib and pulseless V-tach are shockable rhythms. Asystole and PEA are non-shockable. Know the ACLS algorithms for each.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.vomitingCausesM: Vomiting causes metabolic alkalosis (loss of stomach acid). Diarrhea causes metabolic acidosis (loss of bicarbonate from intestines). This distinction is commonly tested.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.waterFollowsSod: "Water follows sodium" — remember that sodium problèmes are often water problèmes. SIADH = dilutional hyponatremia (too much water). Diabetes insipidus = hypernatremia (too little water).
pages.nursingStudyGuides.waterMovesBetwe: Water moves between compartments by osmosis — from zones of lower solute concentration to higher solute concentration. Understanding this principe is essentiel for predicting liquide shifts with IV liquide administration.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.weightGainEdema: Poids gain, œdème (peripheral, periorbital, sacral in bedridden patients), distended neck veins (JVD), crackles/rales activé auscultation, dyspnea, S3 cœur sound, elevated CVP, dilutional hyponatremia, decreased hematocrit.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.wenckebachThink: "Wenck-E-bach" — think "longer, longer, longer, DROP, now you have a Wenckebach." For Type II: "some P
pages.nursingStudyGuides.whenMonitoringI: When surveillance IV magnesium sulfate (e.g., for preeclampsia), assess three things: deep tendon reflexes (should be present), respiratoire évaluer (should be ≥12), and urine sortie (should be ≥30 mL/hr). Absent DTRs = magnesium toxicité.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.whenNotifyingTh: When notifying the fournisseur about a critique Valeur de laboratoire:
pages.nursingStudyGuides.whenYouReceiveA: When you recevoir a critique Valeur de laboratoire, you must: (1) vérifier the résultat, (2) assess the patient, (3) notifier the fournisseur, (4) implement interventions, and (5) document the communication and actions taken.
pages.nursingStudyGuides.wideBizarreQrsC: Wide, bizarre QRS complexes followed by compensatory pause. Occasional PVCs are courant and benign. Frequent PVCs (> 6/min), multifocal, R-activé-T phenomenon, or runs of PVCs exiger intervention (amiodarone, lidocaine, adresse Électrolytes).

pages.perioperativeHubPages.surgicalSite: Chirurgical site infections (SSIs) are among the most courant soins de santé-associated infections. Perioperative nurses play a critique rôle in infection prévention through rigorous adherence to sterile technique and Fondé sur des preuves protocols.
pages.perioperativeHubPages.theCertified: The Certified Perioperative Infirmier(ère) (CNOR) accréditation is the gold standard for OR infirmier excellence. Offered by the Competency & Credentialing Institute (CCI), the CNOR demonstrates avancé connaissance in perioperative Soins aux patients.

pages.perioperativeHubPages.theWhoSurgic: The WHO Chirurgical Sécurité Checklist has reduced chirurgical mortality by 47% and complications by 36% in studies worldwide. Mastering each phase is essentiel for perioperative Pratique infirmière and CNOR examen succès.

pages.pharmacologyHub.expectQuestionsAbo: Expect questions about patients who fail to disclose herbal supplément use. The correct réponse involves asking spécifique, ouvrir-ended questions.
pages.pharmacologyHub.herbalSupplementIn: Herbal supplément interactions, perioperative sécurité, the 4 Gs saignement mnemonic, CYP450 interactions, and patient teaching.
pages.pharmacologyHub.herbalSupplementsA: Herbal supplements are pharmacologically actif and are NOT FDA-regulated. Always assess for interactions.
pages.pharmacologyHub.holdBetablockersIf: Hold beta-blockers if HR < 60 or SBP < 90. Hold digoxin if HR < 60. Hold ACE inhibitors if potassium > 5.0.
pages.pharmacologyHub.ivFurosemideWorksI: IV furosemide works in 5 minutes; PO takes 30-60 minutes. Know when to reassess.
pages.pharmacologyHub.mechanismfirstDrug: Mécanisme-premier médicament explorer. Understand why Effets secondaires happen at the receptor niveau and prédire clinique effects from premier principes.
pages.pharmacologyHub.naloxoneForOpioids: Naloxone for opioids, protamine for heparin, vitamin K for warfarin, flumazenil for benzodiazepines, calcium gluconate for hyperkalemia.
pages.pharmacologyHub.ololBetablockerPri: -olol = beta-blocker, -pril = ACE inhibitor, -sartan = ARB, -statin = HMG-CoA reductase inhibitor, -pam/-lam = benzodiazepine.

pages.pharmacologyHub.potassiumFatalArrh: Potassium (fatal arrhythmia), vancomycin (Red Man Syndrome). Heparin: never IM.

pages.pharmacologyHub.theBestAnswerIsAlw: The meilleur réponse is always the one that ensures the patient understands why a médicament or precaution is important, not just what to do.
pages.pharmacologyHub.warfarinPtinrExtri: Warfarin = PT/INR (extrinsic cheminement). Heparin = aPTT (intrinsic cheminement). Never mix these up.

components.homeBottomSections.readinessE: Twenty-five items with an instant breakdown: see whether timing, knowledge, or judgement slipped before you sit for real.
components.homeBottomSections.reportCard: Category accuracy and trends over time—so you stop re-drilling chapters you already own and focus where score variance is highest.
components.homeBottomSections.studyPlann: Queue blocks from your exam date and the categories that still miss—so after a shift you open the planner and see what to do next, not a blank calendar.
components.homeBottomSections.studyTools: Reports, practice exams, and planners in one learner shell
After a shift, you should not rebuild a study stack from memory. Open the planner for your exam window, run a practice exam when you are ready, and read the report card to see what to queue next.
components.homeConversionSections.compet: Smaller banks, often under a few thousand items
Flashcards sold separately or missing entirely
Dedicated RPN, RN, NP, and allied tracks with correct scope language
Single-exam focus or mixed stems that blur delegation rules
Questions, decks, practice exams, and lessons referencing the same miss patterns
RT, lab, paramedic, imaging, and related tracks with protocol-level framing
Nursing-only content with no credible allied expansion
Category accuracy and session trends that suggest what to queue next
Score-only dashboards with no line-of-sight to the next study block
Most tools optimize for one test. NurseNest keeps questions, decks, practice exams, lessons, and category analytics on one rail so you are not rebuilding a workflow every semester.
components.homeConversionSections.conver: Create a free account — explore the bank
What you actually get in the shell

Fewer open tabs. NurseNest combines {{questions}} exam-style items, {{flashcards}} flashcards across {{decks}} decks, CAT-style practice exams, image-backed items where available, and rationales that spell out safe vs unsafe options—in one study environment aligned to nursing, NP, and allied exams.
components.homeConversionSections.featur: Learn and test modes with spaced scheduling so weak concepts resurface before you forget them.
Full-length timed sessions with breakdowns that show whether timing, knowledge, or judgement slipped.
Strict mode · Score trends · Review queue
{{count}} items across domains with detailed rationales focused on judgement—not trivia recall.
Every option explains the clinical call—so you rehearse elimination logic, not pattern matching.
Pass-focused tools—not a generic content marketplace
Pick the lane that matches tonight’s study goal: drill items, rehearse a practice exam, or read rationales for what you just missed.
components.homeConversionSections.finalC: Start rehearsing with your pathway unlocked
Create a free account to browse starter content. Upgrade when you want the full bank, practice exams, and history for your tier—no surprise charges to look around.
components.homeConversionSections.howItW: How rationales, practice, and progress connect
Three loops you repeat until exam day—each one tied to how boards actually score judgement.
components.homeConversionSections.howSte: Short lessons and reviews organized by system and competency so you know why a presentation matters—not just the buzzword.
Timed and untimed sets, SATA, and case-style stems with rationales that dismantle every option—not just the letter the key lists.
See accuracy by domain, carry results into the next session, and stop guessing what to open after a 12-hour shift.
components.homeConversionSections.platfo: Les captures d'écran de la plateforme ne sont pas disponibles pour le moment. Veuillez réessayer plus tard.
Découvrez la plateforme en action
De l'analytique adaptative à l'entraînement type examen, explorez l'intérieur de NurseNest.
Aperçu de la plateforme
Des indications en temps réel sur la préparation aident à repérer les forces, les points faibles et les prochaines étapes.
Voyez exactement où vous en êtes
Des répartitions par domaine permettent de concentrer le temps d'étude là où c'est le plus utile.
Ciblez plus vite les points faibles
Questions réalistes de type examen (soins infirmiers), séries chronométrées et reprise détaillée des raisonnements.
Entraînement type examen
Le suivi intelligent des cartes aide à réviser les notions clés et à renforcer la mémoire.
Cartes mémoire à répétition espacée
Des études de cas interactives relient données patient, priorisation et prise de décision infirmière.
Renforcez le jugement clinique
Des vues comparatives montrent la progression entre les séances et les difficultés récurrentes.
Suivez l'amélioration dans le temps
Tendances de score, performance en centiles et résultats par catégorie.
Analyses détaillées de séance
Des plans hebdomadaires transforment les points faibles en étapes concrètes.
Plans d'étude personnalisés

components.homeDifferentiation.defaultHe: Certification practice that stays in protocol edges, not trivia lists
Certification prep tied to reasoning checkpoints, not generic flashcards
What you get beyond a static PDF question bank
NP prep that keeps case complexity high without losing the rationale loop
components.homeDifferentiation.defaultSu: NurseNest is built for the judgement calls licensing exams measure: unsafe options, delegation limits, and the pathophysiology behind a symptom—not marketing checklists.
components.homeDifferentiation.grid.adap: Sessions react to what you just missed instead of feeding endless items you already nail—closer to how CAT-style exams probe weak domains.
Difficulty that tracks your miss patterns
components.homeDifferentiation.grid.clin: Each stem explains why the correct choice is safest and why the distractors fail—so you rehearse the same elimination logic the boards reward.
components.homeDifferentiation.grid.inte: Jump from a flagged topic into a lesson, then back into a timed block without exporting CSVs or juggling three subscriptions.
Questions, lessons, practice exams in one rail
components.homeDifferentiation.grid.mult: PN, RN, NP, and allied banks use the right delegation language and risk framing for that registration—not recycled stem banks with a new label.
components.homeDifferentiation.grid.read: See where accuracy wobbles by system or competency, then queue the next session against that bucket instead of guessing what to open tonight.
Category signals you can act on
components.homeDifferentiation.grid.spac: Cards resurface weak concepts on a schedule so pharmacology and labs stick without a separate deck app.
Flashcards tied to what you actually miss
components.homeHeroCarousel.slide01.capt: Des items réalistes façon NCLEX avec des énoncés clairs, des choix de réponse et une interface pensée pour les révisions chronométrées.
components.homeHeroCarousel.slide02.capt: Contenu d’étude approfondi avec des explications reliant physiopathologie et décisions au lit du patient.
components.homeHeroCarousel.slide03.capt: Jeux à fort rendement pour réviser définitions, bilans et médicaments — optimisés pour de courtes sessions.
components.homeHeroCarousel.slide04.capt: Suivez les performances par thème et difficulté pour savoir quoi revoir avant le jour J.
components.homeHeroCarousel.slide05.capt: Cas de type Next Generation qui reflètent priorisation, délégation et jugement clinique en plusieurs étapes.
components.homeHeroCarousel.slide06.capt: Choisissez votre région et votre parcours d’examen pour que contenu, formulations et préparation restent alignés avec votre régulateur.
components.homeHeroCarousel.slide07.capt: Découvrez comment les parcours RN, RPN/infirmier(e) auxiliaire et IP mènent aux bonnes banques, leçons et options.
components.homeHeroCarousel.slide08.capt: Un seul espace pour kiné, ergothérapie, laboratoire et autres certifications paramédicales aux côtés des parcours infirmiers — mêmes habitudes, un compte.
components.homeHeroCarousel.slide09.capt: Étudiez dans la langue et le contexte dont vous avez besoin, avec un contenu adapté aux cohortes internationales.
components.homeHeroCarousel.slide10.capt: Votre point central pour les séries, les révisions dues et la suite entre leçons et questions.
components.homeHeroCarousel.slide11.capt: Un parcours rapide de l’inscription à votre premier lot de pratique — peu de friction, plus de temps sur les questions.
components.homeHeroCarousel.slide12.capt: Résumés de session et détails des scores pour voir forces, lacunes et tendances dans le temps.
components.homeHeroCarousel.slide13.capt: Parcourez des modules structurés par système et par thème pour combler les lacunes entre les blocs de questions.
components.homeHeroCarousel.slide14.capt: Mises en page lisibles sur téléphone et tablette pour les trajets et les pauses au chevet.
components.homeHeroCarousel.slide15.capt: Questions, leçons, cartes et analyses dans un même écosystème premium — sans jongler entre des dizaines d’onglets.

home.conversion.proof.comparethree: Lessons and weak-area signals tied to your track, not a one-size library.
home.comparison.disclaimer: We are not affiliated with other prep companies; this compares product design patterns, not endorsement or item-level equivalence.
home.comparison.highlightGuided: Guided system: bank items → pathway lessons → practice exams, with miss patterns suggesting what to queue next.
home.comparison.highlightReadiness: Readiness: topic and category signals from graded work and practice exams—an internal index, not a pass guarantee.
home.comparison.hubLink: How NurseNest differs from drill-only banks →
home.comparison.pricingLink: Why NurseNest vs. drill-first banks (opens homepage comparison)
home.comparison.row.adaptive.label: Adaptive exams
home.comparison.row.adaptive.nn: Pathway-scoped CAT-style sessions and timed practice exams live beside your bank attempts, with saved history in one account.
home.comparison.row.adaptive.typical: Practice is often fixed-length quizzes; adaptive depth and how it ties to lessons varies widely by vendor.
home.comparison.row.clarity.label: Clarity of scope
home.comparison.row.clarity.nn: Hub, bank, lessons, and exams share one pathway and country context, so RN, PN, NP, and allied wording stay separated by design.
home.comparison.row.clarity.typical: Learners often pair a bank with separate videos, notes, or PDFs; it is easy to drift across scopes if filters are loose.
home.comparison.row.guided.label: Guided study system
home.comparison.row.guided.nn: A repeatable loop—questions, targeted lessons, then practice exams—with miss patterns feeding what to open next. You still choose when to study.
home.comparison.row.guided.typical: Banks reward volume; next-step guidance is usually your own calendar and topic list.
home.comparison.row.integrated.label: Lessons + questions together
home.comparison.row.integrated.nn: Lesson catalogs and the bank use the same pathway keys; topic links and miss tags connect review to the right lesson, fewer tabs.
home.comparison.row.integrated.typical: Lessons and items are often separate purchases or sites, so remediation means manual searching.
home.comparison.row.readiness.label: Readiness tracking
home.comparison.row.readiness.nn: Category and topic signals plus an internal readiness view from graded work and practice—we do not publish pass-rate promises.
home.comparison.row.readiness.typical: Dashboards often emphasize one overall percent or usage time, with limited linkage to what to queue next.
home.comparison.row.simplicity.label: Simplicity of workflow
home.comparison.row.simplicity.nn: One workspace replaces a self-assembled stack of bank + spreadsheets + files for most study sessions.
home.comparison.row.simplicity.typical: You assemble your own rotation from topic menus and external notes; the tool rarely suggests the next block from your last session.
home.comparison.sub: Major banks such as UWorld or Archer are known for large item sets. NurseNest is built as a full nursing licensure study system—pathway-scoped lessons, bank, adaptive and timed practice, and readiness signals in one place. The difference is integration and guidance, not a claim about which bank has more items.
home.comparison.title: Compared with high-volume NCLEX-style banks

home.landing.reviews.card1.quote: NCLEX-RN practice and the lesson for that topic stay in one flow. When I miss an item, the rationale walks through why each option lives or dies, not just the correct letter.
home.landing.reviews.card2.quote: REx-PN language matches how we chart and delegate in school. I am not mentally rewriting US NCLEX-PN stems to fit Canadian scope.
home.landing.reviews.card3.quote: After a timed block I could see I was rushing pharmacology. I used the weak-area cues to drill there instead of rereading whole units for one gap.
home.landing.reviews.card4.quote: NP prep stays in my population here. I am not grinding through cases that will never show up on the board I registered for.
home.landing.reviews.card5.quote: I run a question set, then flashcards on the same system, without opening three apps. Twenty focused minutes beats an hour of passive notes for me.
home.landing.reviews.card6.quote: I quit screenshotting stems into my notes app. Questions, rationales, and quick review all live in one place, so I actually revisit what I got wrong.
home.landing.reviews.disclaimer: Composite, anonymized study scenarios for illustration—not paid testimonials or guaranteed outcomes.
home.landing.reviews.sub: Real study routines: rationales, lessons, flashcards, timed exams, and region-specific wording tied to the license people are sitting.

learner.exams.preset.fullSection.intro: Tirages multi-thèmes avec bandes de difficulté équilibrées, puis brassage final. Les examens RN complets mettent l’accent sur la priorisation, les situations instables et la délégation ; les examens PN sur les situations stables, les tâches et le champ d’exercice. Les abonnés RN/NP peuvent aussi ouvrir la pratique PN complète via l’échelle de paliers.

learner.account.focusAreas.lockedBody: Subscribe to see prioritized weak areas and reinforcement topics.
learner.account.focusAreas.emptyTitle: We need a bit more data
learner.account.focusAreas.emptyBody: Complete a short bank block or a practice test; topic focus lists appear once we have graded attempts.
learner.account.focusAreas.weakLead: Prioritize these with short drills, then revisit lessons if concepts still feel fuzzy.
learner.account.focusAreas.strongLead: Keep mixing these into rotation so strengths stay exam-ready under pressure.
learner.account.reviewQueue.title: Review queue
learner.account.reviewQueue.intro: Turn misses into reps: we prioritize topics from your weak-area ledger—no separate bookmark list required.
learner.account.reviewQueue.lockedBody: Subscribe to unlock topic-level review queues powered by your performance data.
learner.account.reviewQueue.howBody: Incorrect and low-accuracy topics feed your weak-area model. Use drills to clear them systematically; saved-question lists may be added later—today, your data drives the queue.
learner.account.reviewQueue.topicQueueSu: Jump into a targeted drill for any topic below.
learner.account.reviewQueue.emptyQueue: No weak topics detected yet—answer a few more graded questions and check back.
learner.account.crossLinks.title: Related next steps
learner.account.crossLinks.reportCard.le: Turn weak signals into practice: drill topics, review lessons, then check readiness.
learner.account.crossLinks.readiness.lea: Move from this view into your planner, bank, or dashboard when you are ready to study.
learner.account.crossLinks.progress.lead: Continue where you left off and cross-check your report card.
learner.account.crossLinks.billing.lead: Upgrade on pricing, manage Stripe billing below, or return to studying.
learner.account.crossLinks.settings.lead: Jump back to studying or review subscription from the same account hub.
learner.account.overview.title: Account overview
learner.account.overview.intro: Your subscription, performance, and study tools in one place. Use the sidebar to jump to a section.
learner.dashboard.title: Dashboard
learner.dashboard.subtitle.locked: Subscribe to unlock the full question bank, lessons, mocks, and readiness tracking.
learner.dashboard.subtitle.subscriber: Momentum, pathways, and practice signals — then open Account for billing and preferences.
learner.dashboard.commandCenter.title: Your study snapshot
learner.dashboard.commandCenter.subtitle: Real numbers from your plan: streak, bank accuracy, lesson pool, and mock attempts.
learner.dashboard.commandCenter.streakHi: UTC days with lessons, graded work, or mocks
learner.dashboard.commandCenter.bankHint: Finish graded bank or mock items to unlock accuracy
learner.dashboard.commandCenter.lessonsH: Completed vs lessons available in your plan
learner.dashboard.readinessBandExplain.i: We are still collecting graded practice and mock data. Complete a few more timed blocks so this score reflects your true range.
learner.dashboard.readinessBandExplain.n: Signals show gaps versus exam-day expectations. Prioritize weak-topic drills and rationales before long marathon sessions.
learner.dashboard.readinessBandExplain.i: Trajectory is positive—keep pairing new questions with short review of misses so gains stick.
learner.dashboard.readinessBandExplain.n: You are close: tighten consistency on your lowest topics and validate with full mocks under time pressure.
learner.dashboard.readinessBandExplain.r: Practice and mock signals align with strong exam-day readiness. Maintain mix of review and fresh items as your date approaches.
learner.dashboard.categoryBreakdown.hint: Bar colors: strong (green) → on track (blue) → developing (amber) → needs focus (rose).
learner.dashboard.recentPerformance.subt: Trend from your graded sessions and mocks.
learner.dashboard.recentPerformance.noAc: Complete a graded bank block or mock to unlock session accuracy here.
learner.dashboard.recentPerformance.noTr: A bit more activity will unlock a plain-language trend line.
learner.dashboard.examHistory.title: Exam history
learner.dashboard.examHistory.subtitle: Latest mock attempts — watch the trend, not one score.
learner.dashboard.performanceTrend.chart: Percent correct on full mocks (5+ items), oldest to newest.
Complete at least two scored mocks with five or more questions each to see your line chart.
Line chart of mock exam scores: {{points}}
learner.dashboard.progressSummary.title: Progress summary
learner.dashboard.progressSummary.subtit: How your lesson pool and bank work are tracking.
learner.dashboard.progressSummary.streak: Start a streak — any study day counts
learner.dashboard.quickActions.title: Quick actions
learner.dashboard.quickActions.subtitle: Jump back into practice, lessons, or your reports.
learner.dashboard.masteryKey.title: How to read topic strength
learner.adv.strip.card1.title: Learn, then drill
learner.adv.strip.card1.body: Pathway lessons connect to your question bank and weak-area signals—study content and practice stay aligned.
learner.adv.strip.card2.title: Adaptive practice
learner.adv.strip.card2.body: Topic-adaptive and weak-pool sessions adjust to your performance—not a static quiz deck.
learner.adv.strip.card3.title: Readiness & trends
learner.adv.strip.card3.body: One score blends lessons, graded practice, and mocks—see what is lifting or limiting you before test day.
learner.dailyGoal.title: Today's goal
learner.dailyGoal.subtitle: Three light credits: practice, adaptive session, or lesson work—finish any mix that fits your day.
learner.dailyGoal.complete: Today's goal is complete. Consistency beats cramming—you're building exam-day stamina.
learner.dailyGoal.encourage: Pick one small action: a short bank block, one lesson step, or a weak-focused practice test. Small wins stack.
learner.dailyGoal.utcNote: Day and streak use UTC so they stay consistent when you travel.
learner.dailyGoal.resumeFallback: Open your lessons hub to pick up where you left off.
learner.dailyGoal.resumeAlt: Or jump into the question bank →
learner.dailyGoal.microReward: Nice—today's goal is in. We'll keep the next step ready when you come back.
learner.dailyGoal.improveTopic: Your data still flags {{topic}}—short, targeted reps beat rereading everything.
learner.dailyGoal.questionsComplete: You have reached today's question target. Steady reps build automatic recall.
learner.dailyGoal.questionsEncourage: Each graded question updates your weak-area signals—short sessions count.
learner.dailyGoal.setGoalLink: Adjust daily target in Personal info →
learner.retention.reminderContinue: When you return, we'll point you to the last lesson or practice test you touched.
learner.retention.reminderWeak: When your data shows a weak area, we surface lessons and drills in Focus below.
learner.retention.streakProtectShort: Your streak is active — earn at least one study credit today (UTC) so it keeps climbing.
learner.retention.personalMinutesHint: You set about {{n}} minutes per session in your profile. Three small credits still move the needle.
learner.retention.continueSub: Jump back to a lesson, a track hub, or your last mock — all from data we already have.
learner.retention.stripStreakHint: Study any day (UTC) to grow your streak.
learner.adaptive.title: Your focus
learner.adaptive.source.ledger: Ranked from your graded question history—no guesswork.
learner.adaptive.source.mixed: Blends your bank ledger with recent mock signals where the ledger is still thin.
learner.adaptive.source.fallback: We need a bit more graded practice to personalize this list.
learner.adaptive.smallWin: {{topic}} is holding steady at about {{rate}}% accuracy—keep mixing in weak-topic reps.
learner.adaptive.emptyState: Answer a few more graded questions or finish a mock; we will surface weak areas and next steps automatically.
learner.sessionInsight.struggle: Most misses in this session clustered around: {{topics}}.
learner.sessionInsight.focusTopicDrill: When you are ready, a short question drill on {{topic}} matches what this run showed.
learner.practiceTest.insight.focus: Next, bias practice toward those topics in the bank, then revisit lessons if anything still feels fuzzy.
learner.practiceTest.results.weakHint: Each topic is grounded in this session’s results—open lessons or a topic drill next.
learner.dashboard.signedOutHint: Sign in to open your dashboard.
learner.dashboard.accountTeaser: Billing, password, exam date, and UI preferences live in Account.
learner.dashboard.insight.readinessSubti: Blended from lessons, graded practice, and mock performance in your plan.
learner.dashboard.insight.scorePending: Score appears after more graded practice or mock attempts.
learner.dashboard.insight.categoryHint: Top topics from your recent graded activity (indicative, not a pass guarantee).
learner.dashboard.insight.categoryEmpty: Answer more graded questions to see topic-level accuracy here.
learner.dashboard.insight.weakHint: From your latest blended performance analysis.
learner.dashboard.insight.weakEmpty: No labeled weak areas yet. Keep practicing—we will surface priorities automatically.
learner.dashboard.insight.recentHint: Newest first (up to five attempts). Links open the full attempt report.
learner.dashboard.insight.recentEmpty: No mocks logged yet. Complete a mock from Practice exams to see scores and links here.
learner.dashboard.hub.readinessDeferHint: Readiness factors and score are summarized in the card above. This section still tracks lesson completion and recent session accuracy.
learner.dashboard.student.activityHint: Mocks, notes, and your study streak.
learner.dashboard.student.activityEmpty: Finish a lesson, a question set, or a mock to see activity here.
learner.account.reportCard.title: Report card
learner.account.reportCard.intro: A single view of graded practice, mock exams, pathway lesson progress, and topic signals. Sections show “not enough data” when we do not have graded items yet.
learner.account.reportCard.lockedBody: Subscribe to log mock attempts and build a report card.
learner.account.reportCard.empty: No attempts yet. Start a mock from Practice exams.
learner.reportCard.bankSessionsDetail: {correct} correct of {total} graded items across {sessions} sessions.
learner.reportCard.bankSessionsNa: No graded bank or exam-session items in your recent history yet. Complete a timed or tutor session to populate this.
learner.reportCard.loadFailed: We could not load your report card right now. Try again shortly.
learner.reportCard.mockNa: No scored mock attempts yet. Start a practice exam to see accuracy here.
learner.reportCard.mockTierNa: No mock attempts grouped by exam track yet.
learner.reportCard.next.catBody: Use full mocks for exam-length rehearsal, or the practice-test builder for adaptive (CAT) runs when your plan includes them.
learner.reportCard.next.lessonsNa: No in-progress lesson detected. Open lessons to continue your pathway.
learner.reportCard.next.questionsNa: Topic-level drill needs a bit more graded history. Open the bank and practice by system.
learner.reportCard.overallEmptyHint: Start with a few graded bank items or a mock — your report card will fill in as you go.
learner.reportCard.pathwaysNa: No pathway lesson progress recorded yet for your subscription scope.
learner.reportCard.practiceTestsNa: No completed practice tests yet. Finish a CAT or fixed-length test to see results here.
learner.reportCard.questionTierNa: Not enough recent graded session items to break down accuracy by question tier (RN / LPN / NP, etc.).
learner.reportCard.recentBankNa: No recent graded sessions with scored items.
learner.reportCard.section.mockTierSub: Groups your mock attempts by the exam’s pathway tier (RN, RPN/LPN, NP, allied).
learner.reportCard.section.mockTrendSub: Weekly average score on mocks when we have attempts in more than one week.
learner.reportCard.section.nextStepsSub: Based on your current progress and weak signals — not a guarantee of exam outcome.
learner.reportCard.section.overallSub: Bank accuracy uses graded items from recent completed sessions; mock accuracy uses scored full-exam attempts.
learner.reportCard.section.pathwaysSub: Lesson completion by pathway — separate from question-bank accuracy.
learner.reportCard.section.practiceTests: Adaptive (CAT) and other practice tests you have completed.
learner.reportCard.section.questionTierS: From your latest graded session items (bank questions), grouped by item tier.
learner.reportCard.section.recentBankSub: Latest completed sessions with scored items.
learner.reportCard.section.strongTopicsS: Topics with stronger accuracy where we have enough attempts to say so.
learner.reportCard.section.trendsSub: Short notes when we detect improving, declining, or stable patterns.
learner.reportCard.section.weakTopicsSub: Higher miss rates where we have graded evidence.
learner.reportCard.strongNa: No strong topics identified yet — keep practicing to build a clearer picture.
learner.reportCard.trendNa: Trend needs mock attempts in at least two different weeks. Keep testing — we will chart it when there is enough spread.
learner.reportCard.weakNa: No weak topics flagged yet from graded history.
learner.account.readiness.title: Passing probability & readiness
learner.account.readiness.intro: A blended signal from lessons, graded practice, and mocks — indicative only, not a pass guarantee.
learner.account.readiness.empty: Complete more graded question bank or mock sessions to unlock a sharper readiness picture.
learner.readinessPage.catNone: No completed CAT-style practice tests yet. When you finish adaptive runs, we show accuracy here for context (the headline index still uses mocks and bank practice in its formula).
learner.readinessPage.catSummary: {n} completed CAT session(s). Average accuracy where scored: {pct}%.
Why this feels different from a question-only bank
This index blends pathway lesson progress, graded bank and mock performance, and topic load from your weak areas—so it reflects study and practice together, not isolated quiz scores.
learner.readinessPage.confidenceExplaine: Confidence reflects how much graded practice and mock history we have, not clinical certainty.
learner.readinessPage.consistencyNote: Mock score spread across attempts is folded into the mock factor when you have multiple exams — wide swings temper the headline index.
learner.readinessPage.formula.lessons: Lesson completion vs lessons available in your plan pool.
learner.readinessPage.formula.mocks: Recent mock exams (attempts with at least five scored items each).
learner.readinessPage.formula.practice: Accuracy on graded items from recent completed question-bank and exam sessions (your plan scope).
learner.readinessPage.formula.renormaliz: If a signal is missing (for example, no mocks yet), its weight is left out and the index is renormalized so you are not penalized with unexplained empty points.
learner.readinessPage.formula.topics: Topic error load from priority weak areas (ledger + sessions).
learner.readinessPage.indexFootnote: This number is a NurseNest readiness index (0–100), not a published passing probability.
learner.readinessPage.noGradedPractice: No graded bank items in recent completed sessions yet.
learner.readinessPage.noMocks: No mock attempts on file yet.
learner.readinessPage.section.catSub: Adaptive (CAT) practice tests you have completed — shown for transparency; the headline score above does not add a separate CAT weight.
learner.readinessPage.section.consistenc: Study streak and how mock variability affects the mock portion of your index.
learner.readinessPage.section.formulaSub: Matches the server-side blend in `computeReadiness` — weights apply when each signal qualifies.
learner.readinessPage.section.mocksSub: Newest scored mocks (same pool used in the mock factor when eligible).
learner.readinessPage.section.signalsSub: Point contributions after your data qualifies for each signal.
learner.readinessPage.section.weakSub: From unified topic performance. Use questions for targeted drills; lessons for structured review.
learner.readinessPage.streakCaption: Consecutive calendar days (UTC) with any study signal in the last 90 days.
learner.readinessPage.weakEmpty: No weak topics surfaced yet. Complete more graded questions so we can prioritize systems for you.
learner.account.progress.title: Progress
learner.account.progress.intro: Pathway completion, practice accuracy, flashcards, and recent mocks in one scrollable view.
learner.progressPage.continueEmpty: No in-progress lesson on file. Open your lesson list to start or resume.
learner.progressPage.continueSub: Pick up the lesson you last touched in your plan scope.
learner.progressPage.examsCardSub: Practice tests (including CAT) and full mock exams — recent scores only.
learner.progressPage.lessonsCardSub: Across content and pathway lessons in your subscription scope.
learner.progressPage.lessonsPoolNote: {{available}} lessons available in your pool (content + pathways).
learner.progressPage.pathwaysEmpty: No pathway rows for your plan yet. When pathways publish for your tier and region, cards will appear here.
learner.progressPage.pathwaysSub: RN, RPN/LPN, NP, and allied tracks you can access — lesson counts per pathway catalog.
learner.progressPage.questionsAccuracyHi: Correct ÷ attempts in your topic stats ledger.
learner.progressPage.questionsCardSub: Topic ledger totals plus your latest graded exam sessions (plan-scoped).
learner.account.billing.title: Subscription & billing
learner.account.billing.intro: Plan status and Stripe Customer Portal when your subscription is billed through Stripe.
learner.billingPage.accessActive: Your subscription is active for this tier and country scope.
learner.billingPage.accessInactive: No active paid subscription on file. You may still have limited trial or free previews where offered.
learner.billingPage.accountToolsSub: Password and legal links. Use Manage billing above for cards and invoices when Stripe is linked.
learner.billingPage.billingIntervalHint: Exact interval is confirmed in Stripe after checkout. Open Manage billing to see your invoice cycle.
learner.billingPage.billingSub: Renewal dates and billing period come from Stripe when we can read them. Nothing here is a legal invoice.
learner.billingPage.cancelAtPeriodEnd: Your subscription is set to cancel at the end of the current period. You keep access until that date.
learner.billingPage.includes.allied.1: Allied health question bank and lessons aligned to your profession context.
learner.billingPage.includes.allied.2: Practice tests and flashcards where included for your tier.
learner.billingPage.includes.common.1: Question bank, graded sessions, and study tools scoped to your subscription country and tier.
learner.billingPage.includes.common.2: CAT-style and fixed-length practice tests when your plan includes them.
learner.billingPage.includes.np.1: NP-level questions, lessons, and exam-style simulations for your track.
learner.billingPage.includes.np.2: Advanced practice content scoped to your pathway where configured.
learner.billingPage.includes.np.3: Readiness and progress views based on your graded activity.
learner.billingPage.includes.nursing.1: NCLEX-oriented lessons and pathway progress for your nursing tier (RN, RPN, or LPN/LVN).
learner.billingPage.includes.nursing.2: Full question bank and topic drills within your plan scope.
learner.billingPage.includes.nursing.3: Mock exams and practice tests compatible with your subscription.
learner.billingPage.interval.unknown: See Stripe Customer Portal for cycle details
learner.billingPage.noSubscriptionRow: No Stripe subscription row stored yet.
learner.billingPage.pathwaysEmpty: Subscribe to unlock pathway hubs for your region, or contact support if you believe this is wrong.
learner.billingPage.planSub: Effective tier and country drive lessons, questions, and exams you can open.
learner.billingPage.policiesSub: Terms, privacy, refunds, and how to reach us.
learner.billingPage.portalError: We could not open the billing portal. Try again shortly or contact support.
learner.billingPage.portalUnavailable: A Stripe customer is not linked to this account yet. Subscribe from Plans & pricing, or contact support if you already paid.
learner.billingPage.renewalHint: Renewal timing appears here after Stripe sync. You can always confirm in the Customer Portal.
learner.billingPage.renewalUnknown: Not available from Stripe right now
Payment is current and your plan matches the access you see in the app. Use Manage billing in Stripe to update cards or download invoices.
learner.billingPage.surface.admin.body: Admin accounts have full access for operations. Billing for personal study plans does not apply here.
learner.billingPage.surface.admin.title: Administrator access
learner.billingPage.surface.cancelled.bo: This subscription is cancelled in our records. If you recently paid again, allow a few minutes for sync or contact support with your reference id below.
learner.billingPage.surface.grace.body: You still have access during a grace window while billing catches up. Update payment in Stripe soon to avoid interruption.
learner.billingPage.surface.grace.title: Grace period
learner.billingPage.surface.inactive.bod: Start a plan from Pricing to unlock the full bank, lessons, and mocks. If you believe you already subscribed, contact support with the reference below.
learner.billingPage.surface.past_due.bod: Stripe reports an overdue payment. Open Manage billing to fix your payment method and restore uninterrupted access.
learner.billingPage.surface.trial.body: You are in a trial window where offered. When the trial ends, subscribe to keep full access.
learner.billingPage.surface.trial.title: Trial access
learner.billingPage.surface.trial_ending: Your trial is close to ending. Choose a plan to continue without losing momentum.
learner.billingPage.verifyFailed: We could not verify subscription status. You can still review this page; try again shortly or contact support.
learner.account.personal.title: Personal information
learner.account.personal.intro: What we store on your learner profile today.
learner.account.personal.supportHint: Need to change your email or merge accounts? Contact support and we will help securely.
learner.account.personal.securityBody: Update your NurseNest password or use email reset from the security page.
learner.personalPage.section.identitySub: How you appear in the app and how we reach you.
learner.personalPage.emailReadOnly: Email is tied to sign-in. To change it, contact support so we can verify you safely.
learner.personalPage.section.regionSub: Scopes lessons, the question bank, and practice to your licensing context.
learner.personalPage.regionLocked: Your active subscription sets country and exam tier. Update them through billing or contact support.
learner.personalPage.regionHint: Changing country or tier updates which exam pathways you can select below.
learner.personalPage.section.pathwaySub: The track we use for lessons, bank scope, and practice defaults.
learner.personalPage.pathwayHint: Options depend on your country and tier. If you change region or tier, the list refreshes automatically.
learner.personalPage.section.studySub: Optional notes we can use to tailor your experience (not shared publicly).
learner.personalPage.dailyQuestionGoalHi: How many bank questions to aim to grade per day (UTC). Leave blank to use the default (20). Range 5–120.
learner.personalPage.dailyQuestionGoalIn: Enter a whole number between 5 and 120, or leave blank for the default.
learner.personalPage.section.examPlanSub: Managed on the study preferences page so dates and targets stay in sync everywhere.
learner.personalPage.saveError: Could not save. Check your connection and try again.
learner.personalPage.entitlementWarning: We could not fully verify subscription status. You can still edit; if something looks wrong, refresh or try again shortly.
learner.account.settings.title: Settings
learner.account.settings.intro: Appearance and language for the app shell (saved on this device).
learner.account.settings.themeBody: Light, dark, or follow your system. Matches the control in the top bar.
learner.account.settings.languageBody: Study tool and account copy. Same list as the header language control.
learner.account.security.title: Security & password
learner.account.security.intro: Keep your NurseNest password strong. OAuth-only accounts can set a password via email reset.
learner.security.section.passwordSub: Uses your current password. After a successful change you will be signed out here and should sign in again.
learner.security.section.resetSub: Same secure flow as “Forgot password” on the sign-in page.
learner.security.resetBody: If you cannot remember your password, we will email a one-time link. The link expires after a short time. If you sign in with Google or another provider only, use this once to create a NurseNest password.
learner.security.sessionExplain: NurseNest keeps you signed in with an encrypted browser cookie (about {{days}} days). It is not shared with other sites. We do not show a list of devices or browsers.
learner.security.sessionOtherDevices: After you change your password, this browser signs out automatically. On any other device or browser, open NurseNest and choose Sign out if you no longer want that session, or simply sign in again when the old session expires.
learner.security.trouble1: Confirm you are using the same email you registered with, or your username if you set one.
learner.security.trouble2: Try an incognito or private window to rule out extensions or old cookies.
learner.security.trouble3: Request a new reset link from the forgot-password page; older links stop working after use or when they expire.
learner.security.trouble4: Make sure your device clock is roughly correct; extreme skew can break some secure flows.
learner.security.noPasswordBefore: This account does not use a NurseNest password yet. Use
learner.security.noPasswordAfter: with your email to set one.
learner.security.passwordPolicyHint: Use at least 8 characters with a letter and a number.
learner.accountActions.billingBody: Update payment method, view invoices, or cancel in Stripe’s secure portal when your plan is billed through Stripe.
learner.accountActions.policiesBodyWithP: Subscription terms, privacy, refunds, and how to reach us. For payment updates or cancellation, use Manage billing in Stripe above.
learner.accountActions.policiesBodyNoPor: Subscription terms, privacy, refunds, and how to reach us. For payment updates or cancellation, use Stripe after you subscribe.
learner.account.studyPreferences.title: Study preferences
learner.account.studyPreferences.intro: Exam date, target pathway, cadence, and allied profession context — used to tune recommendations.
learner.startStudying.ctaBody: Unlock the full bank, lessons, and review tools on your pathway.
learner.startStudying.ctaTitle: Go deeper when you are ready
learner.startStudying.firstLessonEmpty: Lessons for this pathway are on the way. Jump into questions or open your study hub.
learner.startStudying.heroSubtitle: Your pathway is set. Pick up where new subscribers get the fastest win: one lesson, then a short question set.
learner.studySettings.section.appearance: Matches the header controls. Theme and language apply across the learner app.
learner.studySettings.section.pathwaySub: Scopes the question bank and lessons. Edited with your account profile.
learner.studySettings.section.qbankSub: Starting session size and whether new runs feel like study or exam-style review.
learner.studySettings.practiceModeHint: Study: rationales after you check each answer. Exam-style: explanations stay tucked away until you choose to reveal them (closer to test-day pacing).
learner.studySettings.section.practiceEx: Which option we show first when you start a session (you can always pick the other).
learner.studySettings.deviceNote: Stored locally in your browser so the question bank and practice exam pickers open the way you like. Clearing site data resets them.
learner.studySettings.section.examPlanLo: Exam date, study cadence, and target pathway for recommendations are available with an active plan.
learner.studySettings.notificationsBody: We send essential account, billing, and security messages. Marketing email preferences are not configurable in the app yet — contact support if you need changes.

learner.practiceTests.subtitle.subscribe: Configurez la longueur, les thèmes, la difficulté et le mode de sélection. Lancez des sessions chronométrées ou non, avec sauvegarde automatique, reprise des tests incomplets et analyse par thème avec liens vers les points faibles.

learner.qbank.api.accessVerifyFailed: Impossible de vérifier votre accès (échec de contrôle d’abonnement). Actualisez bientôt. C’est en général temporaire, pas un blocage payant.
learner.qbank.api.genericLoadFailure: Impossible de charger les questions. Vérifiez la connexion et réessayez.
learner.qbank.api.noActiveSubscription: Aucun abonnement actif sur ce compte. Choisissez un forfait pour utiliser la banque complète.
learner.qbank.api.previewsExhausted: Les aperçus gratuits sont épuisés. Abonnez-vous pour l’accès complet et les justifications.
learner.qbank.api.signInAgain: Reconnectez-vous pour charger la banque de questions.
learner.qbank.api.subscriberListBlocked: Ce compte ne peut pas ouvrir la liste abonné avec le forfait ou la session actuelle.
learner.qbank.api.tempUnavailable: La banque de questions est temporairement indisponible (base ou service). Réessayez dans un instant.
learner.qbank.discovery.accessVerifyFail: Impossible de vérifier l’accès pour les filtres de thème. Actualisez bientôt.
learner.qbank.discovery.generic: Le menu des thèmes n’a pas pu se charger ; actualisez.
learner.qbank.discovery.notAvailable: La découverte des thèmes n’est pas disponible pour ce compte ou ce forfait.
learner.qbank.discovery.serverIssue: La liste des thèmes n’a pas pu se charger (serveur ou base temporairement indisponible).
learner.qbank.discovery.signIn: Connectez-vous pour charger les filtres par thème.
learner.qbank.discovery.subscriptionRequ: Le menu des thèmes nécessite un abonnement actif ; les aperçus restent disponibles ailleurs.
learner.qbank.examUi.enrichmentNotice: Rationale available but needs enrichment. We’re expanding explanations across the bank.
learner.qbank.examUi.noRationale: No explanation is on file for this item yet.
learner.qbank.examUi.toolsHint: Use ∅ to strike out and H to highlight while you think—optional, like paper.
learner.qbank.rationale.wrongOptionsHead: Why the other options are wrong
learner.qbank.rationale.verdictCorrectSu: Nice work—read the rationale below to reinforce the concept.
learner.qbank.rationale.verdictIncorrect: Review the correct reasoning and compare it to your choice.
learner.qbank.rationaleLinks.heading: Related lessons
learner.qbank.filters.incorrectOnlyHint: Uses recent history from this browser’s question bank practice. Access rules still apply on the server.
learner.qbank.filters.modeExam: Exam style (hide rationale until you open it)
learner.qbank.filters.noMistakesBody: Answer a few questions first, or turn off “Mistakes only” to load the full bank.
learner.qbank.empty.bankEmptyGlobal.body: Il n’y a pas encore d’items publiés dans la banque. C’est un manque de contenu, pas un problème d’abonnement. Contactez le support si vous attendiez des données.
learner.qbank.empty.default.body: Essayez un autre thème, effacez les filtres ou actualisez. Si vous venez de vous abonner, attendez un instant et réessayez.
learner.qbank.empty.default.title: Aucune question dans cette vue
learner.qbank.empty.entitlementExcludesA: Des questions existent, mais aucune ne correspond à votre pays et à votre palier. Vérifiez le profil et le forfait, ou contactez le support.
Aucune question ne correspond à votre profil
learner.qbank.empty.indeterminate.body: La liste est vide pour une raison inhabituelle. Effacez les filtres, puis actualisez. Si cela persiste, contactez le support avec l’heure de la tentative.
learner.qbank.empty.indeterminate.title: Aucune question dans cette vue
learner.qbank.empty.pathwayNotOnPlan.bod: Ce parcours n’est pas inclus dans votre abonnement ; le filtre n’a pas été appliqué. Effacez le filtre par parcours ou passez à un forfait supérieur. Si la liste est vide, la cause ci-dessous s’applique toujours.
learner.qbank.empty.pathwayNotOnPlan.tit: Parcours non inclus à votre forfait
learner.qbank.empty.pathwayPoolEmpty.bod: Votre abonnement contient des items, mais aucun n’est étiqueté pour ce parcours. Essayez « Tous les thèmes » sans filtre par parcours, ou choisissez un autre parcours.
learner.qbank.empty.pathwayPoolEmpty.tit: Rien pour ce parcours dans votre pool
learner.qbank.empty.topicNoMatch.body: Le filtre correspond exactement au libellé en base. Choisissez un autre thème dans le menu ou « Tous les thèmes ».
learner.qbank.empty.topicNoMatch.title: Aucune question pour ce libellé de thème
learner.qbank.empty.unknownPathway.body: « {{pathwayId}} » n’est pas un identifiant de parcours reconnu ; la banque n’a pas été filtrée. Retirez le filtre par parcours ou choisissez un parcours depuis votre hub.
learner.qbank.empty.unknownPathway.title: Parcours inconnu
learner.qbank.loading: Chargement de votre banque de questions…
learner.qbank.networkError: Erreur réseau lors du chargement des questions.
learner.qbank.notice.topicMenuTruncated: Affichage des {{cap}} thèmes les plus fréquents dans votre banque ({{omitted}} questions supplémentaires dans des thèmes non listés). Choisissez « Tous les thèmes » en pratique par thème pour parcourir les regroupements de découverte.
learner.qbank.notice.topicRelaxed: Aucune correspondance exacte pour le thème « {{topic}} ». Affichage des questions de votre parcours. Utilisez le menu des thèmes pour affiner.
learner.qbank.notice.weakLowConfidence: Le rattachement au thème (points faibles) est peu fiable pour le moment. Continuez à noter des items pour affiner l’alignement.
learner.qbank.notice.weakUnavailable: Le mode points faibles nécessite d’abord quelques réponses notées. Nous affichons un pool mixte en attendant le registre des thèmes.
learner.qbank.pickTopic.body: Choisissez un thème dans le menu ci-dessous pour un quiz par thème (items récents d’abord).
learner.qbank.pickTopic.title: Choisissez un thème
learner.qbank.ui.batchProgressNote: La progression de ce lot est enregistrée dans votre navigateur pour que vous puissiez actualiser et continuer. Les modes aléatoire et mélangé excluent les questions déjà vues cette session lorsque vous chargez plus. La notation est effectuée côté serveur. Les réponses ne sont pas notées uniquement dans la page.
learner.qbank.ui.examShellHint: Session type examen : les explications restent masquées jusqu’à votre choix, comme un rythme sans notes (entraînement uniquement).
learner.qbank.ui.learningHigh: Forte correspondance aux métadonnées de l’item. Les prochaines étapes devraient rester sur la même piste thématique.
learner.qbank.ui.learningLow: Attribution thématique approximative seulement. Ouvrez la banque de questions et ajustez les filtres si les résultats semblent hors cible.
learner.qbank.ui.learningMedium: Basé sur le thème de la question ; si la banque semble trop large, affinez avec les filtres ou votre file de révision prioritaire.
learner.qbank.ui.sessionLineIdle: Répondez et vérifiez pour suivre cette session
learner.qbank.ui.topicCodeLine: Code thème : {{code}} · confiance d’attribution au thème {{confidence}}
learner.questions.howTo.footer: Les contenus premium et les justifications sont à usage personnel de l’abonné. La copie à l’écran est limitée ; utilisez Mes notes pour le texte imprimable ou exportable.
learner.questions.howTo.integratedLoop: Misses here feed your weak-topic signals and can surface matched pathway lessons on the dashboard—keep lessons and bank practice in the same loop for faster gains.
learner.questions.howTo.li1: Sélectionnez des réponses, puis utilisez Vérifier la réponse pour un retour immédiat.
learner.questions.howTo.li2: Combinez avec les examens d’entraînement pour une répétition chronométrée sur l’ensemble.
learner.questions.howTo.li3a: Pour une session plus proche de l’examen (explications masquées tant que vous ne choisissez pas), ajoutez
learner.questions.howTo.li3b: à l’URL de cette page, ou utilisez
learner.questions.howTo.title: Comment utiliser cette banque
learner.questions.subtitle.locked: Entraînement avec justifications après chaque item, filtres par thème et reprise de session, inclus avec un forfait actif.
learner.questions.subtitle.subscriber: Répondez aux QCM et SATA, vérifiez contre la clé officielle et lisez les justifications. Utilisez le filtre par thème pour cibler les points faibles. La progression de cette session est enregistrée dans votre navigateur.

learner.pathway.missingBanner.title: Set your exam pathway to unlock pathway filters
learner.practiceTests.historyEmpty: No saved practice tests yet. Build one above, or warm up in the question bank first.

learner.studyPlan.subtitle.subscriber: Votre date d’examen, votre rythme et vos données de pratique déterminent les objectifs hebdomadaires et les jalons. Une structure hebdomadaire optionnelle par IA est disponible ci-dessous. Activez avec `AI_STUDY_PLAN_ENABLED=true` lorsque c’est configuré. Ce n’est pas un avis médical.

pages.alliedHealthHub.footerNursingPathw: Looking for NCLEX or NP instead? Browse nursing and advanced practice pathways.
pages.alliedHealthHub.heroH1: Certification prep for your discipline, not a generic nursing page
pages.alliedHealthHub.heroTestimonial: Allied candidates tell us the stems feel closer to registry exams than random quiz banks.
pages.alliedHealthHub.heroValueLine: Practice exam-style questions, review key protocols, and focus on the concepts most likely to appear on your certification exam.
pages.alliedHealthHub.heroWorkflowLine: Start with a topic, review the concept, then apply it with practice questions and timed sets.
pages.alliedHealthHub.metaDescription: Certification prep for respiratory therapy, medical laboratory, imaging, paramedic, pharmacy technician, rehabilitation, and related allied credentials. Choose your country and profession. Lessons and practice stay on the allied subscription tier.
pages.alliedHealthHub.metaTitle: Allied health certification prep | RT, MLT, paramedic & more | NurseNest
pages.alliedHealthHub.professionsIntro: Each track pairs a prep guide with a paginated lesson hub. Questions, lessons, and timed sets stay inside the allied tier, separate from NCLEX and NP depth.
pages.alliedHealthHub.regionCanadaPricin: Checkout uses Canadian billing and the allied subscription tier.
pages.alliedHealthHub.regionIntro: Allied content is scoped by country at signup and checkout. Pick your region first, then your profession below.
pages.alliedHealthHub.regionUnitedStates: Checkout uses US billing and the allied subscription tier.
pages.alliedHealthHub.trustSub: Confidence cues before you commit to a plan.
pages.alliedHealthHub.trustTile1Body: Pick your profession and land on guides and lessons written for that scope, not generic nursing copy.
pages.alliedHealthHub.trustTile2Body: United States and Canada each have a dedicated allied hub so entitlements and checkout match your exam market.
pages.alliedHealthHub.trustTile3Body: Lessons, questions, and practice stay inside the allied plan you select. Upgrade paths are explicit, not mixed in by accident.
pages.alliedHealthHub.whoBody: Built for allied health certifications such as respiratory therapy, paramedic, medical laboratory, imaging, and rehabilitation pathways.
pages.examLessons.appLessonsLead: Vous cherchez la liste des leçons dans l’application (abonné) ?
pages.examLessons.emptyPreviewTopicsHint: Open the hub for the full lesson list and previews for this pathway.
pages.examLessons.intro: Chaque hub ci-dessous correspond à un pays, un titre et un examen. Pas de mélange de terminologie ni de fuite entre spécialités NP lorsque vous définissez votre filière. Commencez par un aperçu, puis débloquez la leçon complète avec le forfait adapté.
pages.examLessons.metaDescription: Parcourez des leçons infirmières par pays, rôle et examen (REx-PN, NCLEX-RN, NCLEX-PN, spécialités NP, etc.). Les aperçus sont gratuits ; la profondeur complète se débloque avec l’abonnement correspondant.

pages.forInstitutions.description: Licences par volume et accès de cohorte pour écoles de soins infirmiers, programmes et équipes de formation en santé utilisant NurseNest Core.
pages.forInstitutions.h1: Tarification institutionnelle pour programmes et écoles
pages.forInstitutions.intro: Offrez à votre cohorte la même préparation NCLEX-RN, REx-PN, NP et professions para médicales que les abonnés individuels — avec des sièges, des options de déploiement et des conditions adaptées à votre programme.
pages.forInstitutions.nextBody: Indiquez le type de programme, le nombre approximatif d’apprenants et l’échéance — nous répondrons avec des options de tarification et de déploiement. Pour un seul apprenant, les forfaits individuels restent sur la page Tarifs.
pages.forInstitutions.offer1: Licence par sièges alignée sur la taille de cohorte et la durée du trimestre
pages.forInstitutions.offer2: Paliers alignés sur les filières (RPN/LVN, RN, NP, professions para médicales) comme les offres individuelles publiques
pages.forInstitutions.offer3: Parcours d’inscription (vérification de domaine ou codes d’invitation) selon votre entente
pages.forInstitutions.offer4: Contrôles d’accès centralisés — le même modèle côté serveur que les abonnements individuels

pages.pricing.faq.0.a: Plans are sold by pathway tier (for example RN, PN, NP, or Allied) and country. After purchase, that tier unlocks the matching question bank, lessons, and practice modes for that registration context.
pages.pricing.faq.1.a: Yes. NurseNest uses Stripe for checkout and subscription management. You can update payment methods and cancel from the billing portal.
pages.pricing.faq.2.a: Refunds follow the subscription and refund policy linked at checkout. If you have a billing issue, contact support with your account email.
pages.pricing.faq.3.a: Your bank stays scoped to the tier you bought. Switching tracks usually means a new plan that matches the new pathway.
pages.pricing.faq.3.q: Can I change exam tracks later?
pages.pricing.faq.4.a: Use timed sets when your accuracy is stable on topics, then full practice exams for pacing. The app highlights weak categories so your next block targets risk, not comfort.
pages.pricing.faq.4.q: How should I use practice exams?
pages.pricing.faqSection.title: Pricing questions

pages.pricing.hero.trustLine: Transparent pricing: you always know which pathway tier you are activating before checkout.
pages.pricing.institutionalBanner: Programmes et écoles : licences par volume, sièges de cohorte et déploiement simplifié — voir la tarification institutionnelle.
pages.pricing.intro: Choisissez votre filière et votre pays. Après paiement, ce palier débloque la banque complète, les leçons, les sessions chronométrées et l’historique des scores pour votre contexte d’inscription — sans mélanger le contenu d’autres champs d’exercice.

pages.pricing.micro.marks: Missed items become review targets, not one-off mistakes.
pages.pricing.micro.worry: Most people do not fail from lack of content. They fail from unfocused repetition.

pages.pricing.social.passRateLine: Les apprenants qui gardent un rythme hebdomadaire — questions, justifications, blocs chronométrés — se sentent souvent plus stables à l’approche de l’examen. Le résultat dépend de votre utilisation de la banque.

pages.publicLessons.introUS: Browse lessons by pathway, then move directly into practice questions and rationales tied to your exam.
